title: 重命名核心代码符号与服务文案
summary: Renamed core Ralph symbols and service-facing copy to Harness across localization, help/manual generation, runtime messaging, prompt contracts, policy/task types, workspace path helpers, and related tests while preserving the .harness-runner artifact root and legacy compatibility cleanup for old ralph-runner settings keys.
changedModules: extension runtime orchestration and task-state manager
localization packs and user-facing service copy
help document builders and document kinds
project constraint prompting and editable-title generation
task memory, checkpoint, and evidence synthesis fallbacks
workspace path helpers and source-context scaffolding
policy/task shared types and policy gate helpers
knowledge coverage hints, run-log classification, and prompt contracts
architectureNotes: The persistence boundary stays .harness-runner; symbol and copy rebranding was applied around that stable storage root rather than moving artifact paths.
Localization, help content, runtime logs, and tests were updated together because brand renames are only safe when user-visible strings and assertions move in lockstep.
Legacy ralph-runner workspace setting cleanup remains intentionally preserved in src/extension.ts as a compatibility seam, separate from the active Harness surface.
Type and helper renames were propagated through policy, task-status, help, and workspace-path modules so extension.ts can consume a consistent Harness-named API surface.
The rollback unit is the combined rename across runtime, prompts, localization, and tests; partial reverts would leave mismatched exported symbols and broken assertions.
keyDecisions: Use Harness as the active product/runtime name in status bar text, help titles, runtime logs, prompt contracts, and synthesized fallback messages.
Rename core exported symbols such as help builders, language-pack helpers, task-state types, policy config types, and workspace-path helpers to Harness-prefixed names.
Keep .harness-runner unchanged as the artifact root and keep deletion of legacy ralph-runner.* workspace settings for backward compatibility.
Update tests with the same pass so renamed titles, prompt copy, and exported helper names remain verifiable under compilation.
constraintsConfirmed: No dist/ or out/ files were hand-edited.
Validation ran through npm run compile and npm run compile-tests.
Compatibility cleanup for legacy ralph-runner workspace settings was preserved.
risks: The checked-in icon asset path and packaged VSIX filename still use legacy ralph naming, so distribution/file branding is not fully normalized.
Some remaining ralph occurrences in tests are compatibility fixtures or temporary directory names rather than active runtime branding and may be intentionally left for later cleanup.
The repository slug in package metadata still references vscode-copilot-ralph-runner and was not changed by this story.
followUps: Decide whether to rename the checked-in icon asset and packaged VSIX filename to match Harness branding.
Audit non-source assets and repository metadata for any remaining intentional or accidental ralph branding.
If desired, run the full npm test suite in a later pass in addition to compile and compile-tests.

dimensions.label: Architecture Consistency
dimensions.summary: Core rename surfaces and tests are aligned.

dimensions.label: Acceptance Coverage
dimensions.summary: Acceptance rename targets are reflected in code and strings.

dimensions.label: Change Scope Control
dimensions.summary: The story did not alter prd.json or runtime artifact-root paths.

dimensions.label: Verifiability
dimensions.summary: Compile and test-compilation evidence are attached.
